etymology.description: The word 同樣(同样) (tóngyàng, "same") is formed by combining: 同(同) (tóng, "same; together") + 樣(样) (yàng, "appearance; kind; pattern"). 同(同) (tóng, "same") implies similarity or uniformity, while 樣(样) (yàng, "appearance") refers to the form or type. Together, they convey the idea of having the same form or type, hence "same".
etymology.interpretation: 同(同) (tóng, "same; together") signifies similarity, as in collective identity. 樣(样) (yàng, "appearance; kind; pattern") encompasses visual or structural qualities. Combining these, 同樣(同样) (tóngyàng) denotes having the identical appearance or type, hence 'same.' This bridges concepts of likeness and form/pattern.

examples.chinese: 我们喜欢同样的音乐(我們喜歡同樣的音樂)。
他的意见和我的意见同样重要(他的意見和我的意見同樣重要)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de yìjiàn hé wǒ de yìjiàn tóngyàng zhòngyào.
examples.english: His opinion is equally important as mine.

examples.chinese: 这两份工作同样有挑战性(這兩份工作同樣有挑戰性)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè liǎng fèn gōngzuò tóngyàng yǒu tiǎozhàn xìng.
examples.english: Both jobs have the same level of challenge.
